---
title: "A Beuatiful Graphic"
author: "Dhruva Arcot"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  message: false
---
Dhruva Arcot is a data maestro!

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Summarize average price by cut and color
diamonds_summary <- diamonds %>%
  group_by(cut, color) %>%
  summarise(avg_price = mean(price), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(diamonds_summary, aes(x = color, y = avg_price, fill = cut)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.65) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Fair"      = "#b5c4b1",
      "Good"      = "#7fa99b",
      "Very Good" = "#4e8d8d",
      "Premium"   = "#2c6e7e",
      "Ideal"     = "#1a3d5c"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::dollar_format(scale = 1e-3, suffix = "k"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Diamond Prices by Color & Cut",
    subtitle = "Average retail price across GIA color grades D–J",
    x        = "Color Grade  (D = Colorless → J = Near-Colorless)",
    y        = "Average Price",
    fill     = "Cut Quality",
    caption  = "Source: ggplot2 built-in `diamonds` dataset"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "#0f1923", color = NA),
    panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "#0f1923", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "#ffffff18"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    axis.text          = element_text(color = "#c9d4dc"),
    axis.title         = element_text(color = "#8fa8bf", size = 10),
    plot.title         = element_text(color = "#e8f0f7", face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "#8fa8bf", size = 11, margin = margin(b = 12)),
    plot.caption       = element_text(color = "#4a6272", size = 8, hjust = 0),
    legend.background  = element_rect(fill = "#0f1923", color = NA),
    legend.key         = element_rect(fill = NA),
    legend.text        = element_text(color = "#c9d4dc"),
    legend.title       = element_text(color = "#8fa8bf", face = "bold"),
    plot.margin        = margin(20, 24, 16, 20)
  )
```
